quickQuiz.question: Which of these is NOT a phase of the Node.js Event Loop?
options: Poll
Check
Settle
Close Callbacks
quickQuiz.explanation: 'Settle' is not a phase. The main phases are Timers, Pending Callbacks, Idle/Prepare, Poll, Check, and Close Callbacks.

quickQuiz.question: What is the main purpose of using a Stream in Node.js?
options: To make the code run faster.
To process large amounts of data without loading it all into memory.
To allow the server to handle multiple requests at once.
To encrypt data during transmission.
quickQuiz.explanation: Streams process data in chunks, allowing you to handle files larger than the available RAM.

quickQuiz.question: Which method executes a callback IMMEDIATELY after the current operation, before any other event loop phase?
options: setTimeout(fn, 0)
setImmediate()
process.nextTick()
Promise.resolve().then()
quickQuiz.explanation: process.nextTick is the highest priority asynchronous operation in Node.js.

quickQuiz.question: What is the difference between a Worker Thread and a Child Process in Node.js?
options: Worker Threads share memory; Child Processes have their own memory.
Child Processes are faster for CPU-bound tasks.
Worker Threads can only run shell commands.
There is no difference; they are aliases.
quickQuiz.explanation: Worker Threads are lightweight and share memory via SharedArrayBuffer, whereas Child Processes are separate OS processes with their own memory and PID.

quickQuiz.question: If you use `fs.readFileSync` in an Express route handler, what happens to other concurrent requests?
options: They are processed normally in the background.
They are queued and handled after the file is read.
The entire server freezes until the file is read, blocking all other requests.
The server crashes immediately.
quickQuiz.explanation: Synchronous methods block the single main thread of the Event Loop, preventing any other code (including other requests) from executing.

masteryQuiz.explanation: 1. Sync code (1, 6) runs first. 2. nextTick (4) runs immediately after sync. 3. Microtasks/Promises (5) run next. 4. Timers (2) run in the Timers phase. 5. setImmediate (3) runs in the Check phase.

masteryQuiz.question: You are building a real-time log processor that reads a 10GB file and calculates a hash. The server keeps crashing with 'Out of Memory'. How do you fix this?
options: Increase the Node.js heap size using --max-old-space-size.
Use fs.readFile and process the string in chunks.
Implement a readable stream and pipe it into a crypto hash stream.
Use Worker Threads to read the file in parallel.
masteryQuiz.explanation: Piping a ReadStream into a Hash stream ensures that only a small chunk of the file is in memory at any given time, regardless of the total file size.
